_source: Text from https://wiiu.hacks.guide/common-issues-fixes.html — kept locally so errors show in-app.

items.fix: Same as the -3 error: the SD card isn't detected. Check the card isn't locked (the physical switch on the side) and isn't labeled **WIIU** — both cause conflicts. Re-insert it and make sure it's FAT32.
The console can't find the payload. You need **payload.elf** inside the **wiiu** folder, and the **wiiu** folder on the **root** of the SD card.
Missing **homebrew_launcher.elf** in the `/wiiu/apps/homebrew_launcher` folder. Re-copy the Homebrew Launcher files onto the card.
The console sees the card but can't mount it. Reformat it as FAT32, and use a reputable-brand SD card — cheap/counterfeit cards often fail to mount.

"Re-insert the SD Card and try again. Make sure the SD Card is in FAT32 format." If it still fails, clean the SD slot with compressed air. !!Do not blow into it with your mouth!! — moisture can damage the console.

items.error: -4: SD detected but could not mount
items.fix: Make sure the card uses **MBR** partitioning, not GPT. If it has multiple partitions, consolidate them into one.
Haxchi is looking for the Homebrew Launcher at `/wiiu/apps/homebrew_launcher/homebrew_launcher.elf`. Re-copy those files onto the card.

items.error: Error Code 160-0101 when booting up after vWii Decaffeinator
items.fix: Take out the inserted disc and unplug any external drives, then restart the system.

items.error: Error Code 105-3102 when performing the system update
items.fix: Restart your console and reattempt the update.

items.error: A system-memory error has occurred. Turn off the console, and then try again. (while deleting DON'T TOUCH ME)
items.fix: Restart the system and try to delete the application again. Make sure you followed **all** the uninstall steps before deleting it — the Wii U must no longer autoboot into CBHC.

items.error: Data Management asks to delete unnecessary data
items.fix: This is leftover files from incomplete installs. Always choose **Yes** to delete it — it just wastes space. If the deletion loops forever, manually clear the `/storage_mlc/usr/import` folder with FTPiiU Everywhere (or `/storage_usb/usr/import` if you're on USB). The `import` folder should always be empty.

items.error: My HDD doesn't work or makes a clicking sound
items.fix: The Wii U doesn't push enough power through a single USB port to run a hard drive. Use a **powered external drive**, or a **Y-cable** that connects the HDD to two USB ports. Same fix if a drive that used to work suddenly stops.

items.error: Lost online connectivity with an HDD on top of the console
items.fix: The internal Wi-Fi antenna is being interfered with by the hard drive's magnet. Move the drive to a different spot on the console, or off it entirely, to restore the connection.
